evidence: Paper computes Gini importance and ranks variables in Figure 3, stating "GDI plays a much more important role." However, the main conclusions about reduced mobility in low-income and high-gender-inequality contexts rest on OLS regression coefficients (GDPpc_p, GDI_p/c) with p<0.001, not on importance ranking. External validation against UK/IPS data and US income confirms the direction of effects. Impurity bias does not corrupt the significance or direction of the regression-based conclusions, though it may overstate the relative importance of GDI vs. gender categorical variable.
